summary: Development Dossier DD-01 from the Feline Superiority Lab (July 2026) covers team composition, IP status, manufacturing/CMC considerations, regulatory pathway, and development milestones for a non-therapeutic companion-animal platform (cats). The platform targets companionship-deficit and household-noise-sensitivity contexts for knowledge workers, with an addressable population of ~370M cat-owning households globally. The project is at an early research/behavioral-study stage with one completed cohort study and several planned expansions.

content_blocks.title: Key Personnel & Affiliations
content_blocks.content: Principal Investigator: Dr. M. Whiskerton, DVM/PhD (behavioral ethology), Molecule DeSci Companion-Animal Group. Co-Investigator: Dr. A. Purrsson (bioacoustics), Institute for Domestic Feline Studies, Zurich. Data & Biostatistics: R. Tabbyani, MSc. Program Operations: Molecule Labs. Team expertise spans in vivo behavioral trial design, acoustic instrumentation, and reproducible-research tooling.

content_blocks.title: Intellectual Property Status
content_blocks.content: One provisional patent application filed (2026-05) covering a 'method for metered-affection companionship delivery and acoustic-footprint minimization' — method-of-use claims only. Composition-of-matter claims not pursued (subject is a naturally occurring platform). Preliminary FTO review found no blocking patents; a prior 1997 filing on automated feeders was assessed non-blocking (different claim scope).

content_blocks.title: Manufacturing / CMC & Delivery Considerations
content_blocks.content: Platform is self-replicating and self-manufacturing; GMP not applicable. Delivery route (adoption + acclimatization) assessed as low-friction and high-viability. Scale-up constraint: supply is shelter-limited, not production-limited; partnership with rehoming networks recommended over de novo manufacturing. No CMC blockers identified.

content_blocks.title: Regulatory Pathway
content_blocks.content: No IND-enabling studies initiated (out of scope for a non-therapeutic companion-animal platform). Applicable oversight is animal-welfare and ethics review. IACUC-equivalent approval obtained for the behavioral cohort (protocol FSL-2026-07). No formal toxicology package required or initiated.

content_blocks.title: Development Timeline & Next Milestones
content_blocks.content: Completed milestones: cohort study ER-01 (2026-07), provisional patent filing (2026-05), whitepaper v1.0 (2026-07). Planned M1: expand cohort to n=60 with multi-site replication, target 2026-Q4. Planned M2: longitudinal 6-month retention & satisfaction study, target 2027-Q1. Planned M3: open community-funded replication round and pre-register endpoints, target 2027-H1.

content_blocks.title: Patient / Beneficiary Population
content_blocks.content: Estimated addressable population: ~370M cat-owning households globally (proxy for beneficiary scale). Target 'indication': companionship-deficit and household-noise-sensitivity contexts for the modern knowledge worker.

key_findings: Platform is a non-therapeutic companion-animal system (cats); GMP and IND-enabling studies are explicitly out of scope.
IACUC-equivalent ethics approval obtained for behavioral cohort under protocol FSL-2026-07, indicating institutional oversight is in place.
Cohort study ER-01 completed July 2026 — earliest experimental evidence available; no quantitative efficacy data reported in this dossier.
Provisional patent filed May 2026 on method-of-use claims for 'metered-affection companionship delivery and acoustic-footprint minimization'; no composition-of-matter claims.
Preliminary FTO review found no blocking patents; prior 1997 automated-feeder filing assessed non-blocking.
Scale-up is shelter-supply-limited, not manufacturing-limited; no CMC blockers identified.
Team has relevant expertise: behavioral ethology (PI), bioacoustics (Co-I), biostatistics, and program operations.
Addressable market estimated at ~370M cat-owning households globally.
Project is at an early research/behavioral-study stage (TRL 2–3 equivalent), with planned multi-site replication and longitudinal studies still ahead.
